120100.explanation: It is desirable to have a good balance of men and women in the executive board, but ultimately quality must be decisive. If that leads to more women or men in the board, that need not be a problem.

120101.explanation: In certain cases, that is certainly possible. Think of major decisions such as merging municipalities. For many matters, however, the municipal council is responsible, since they are elected to govern and make decisions in the general interest.

120104.explanation: The hospitality industry is perfectly capable of making agreements with the municipality about opening hours. However, if there is nuisance, the municipality must intervene and impose restrictions. In other words, freedom upfront and enforcement afterward.

120108.explanation: As long as there is no national ban, we do not believe that as a local government we should restrict residents. There are enough challenges for the municipality to tackle.

120111.explanation: This is a strange statement. Free collection does not exist; someone pays those costs. The costs are either paid directly by the applicant or through waste disposal levies. ONS basically supports placing the costs as much as possible on the user.

120117.explanation: The municipality must encourage music education in various ways. Through the sports and culture fund we give direct money to children from low-income families. And by providing affordable practice spaces, we subsidize music education and keep it affordable.

120118.explanation: Of course, a sports club must be financially healthy, but the municipality should support and lend a helping hand wherever possible. Sport is extremely important for the development of children and for creating a social network.

120119.explanation: Generally, monument care is a task of the national government. These are also national monuments, for which there are national subsidies. For municipal monuments there is a small subsidy scheme. ONS is not in favor of abolishing this subsidy.

120121.explanation: Camera surveillance works, so where necessary we will expand it.

120122.explanation: Unfortunately, street violence is becoming increasingly severe. Enforcement officers (BOA’s) who are trained for this must be able to protect themselves and residents.

120129.explanation: Work is the best way to escape poverty and build a social network. If someone cannot work, then meaningful social engagement is a valuable daily activity and a welcome addition to social life.

120130.explanation: Vulnerable residents should receive support and often really need it. The existing exemption from waste disposal charges is, according to ONS, a good concession.

120131.explanation: Of course, ONS wants as much support as possible for vulnerable residents, but the honest truth is that we already have a broad support package, also in comparison to many other municipalities. The honest story is also that this will cost a lot of extra money and therefore lead to higher taxes.

120133.explanation: More and more people will own an electric car in the future, and charging points will increase accordingly. However, care must be taken to ensure that sufficient parking spaces remain to prevent parking problems in the neighborhood.

120134.explanation: Where there is traffic nuisance, certainly, but in many places it is safe to drive 50 km/h, such as on the larger district access roads which are often dual carriageways.

120135.explanation: According to ONS, this should only be considered if residents choose so because parking pressure arises. In the neighborhoods around the center, ONS can imagine expanding the permit parking area, but for the rest of Spijkenisse and the other towns, permit parking is not appropriate.

120137.explanation: Strange statement. In ONS's opinion, the municipality does not decide this, but from our viewpoint, everyone should be treated equally and of course with as much respect as possible for their beliefs. Good care is paramount. Tailor-made care for everyone is unfeasible, unaffordable, and undesirable.

120138.explanation: Price is certainly an important factor, but obviously also quality. Furthermore, it must be assessed whether this care should be offered at all or if there are simply more accessible and affordable alternatives to keep youth care costs under control.

120139.explanation: Residents are primarily responsible for this themselves. When residents cannot afford this, there are schemes to combat poverty and debt. Hygiene products must be accessible to everyone.

120141.explanation: The national government has promised for ten years a tax revision in which the dog tax would be abolished and municipalities would have more room within the tax domain. Now, abolishing it would directly lead to an increase in property tax (OZB) or cutbacks. ONS is not in favor of that.

120143.explanation: It is important that companies can expand to improve employment in Nissewaard and prevent entrepreneurs from leaving.

120145.explanation: This measure will be abolished by the national government, so Nissewaard will have to comply with this as well.

120149.explanation: ONS is not in favor of coffeeshops because the past has shown they cause nuisance and attract dealers of hard drugs.

120152.explanation: Slumlords must be excluded. In addition, there is much nuisance caused by migrant workers who often use this construction. After all, this causes local residents to be unable to find housing because it is very profitable to rent out real estate this way.

120153.explanation: Every town should have a meeting place where activities can be organized and residents can meet each other.

120156.explanation: ONS believes that for large housing projects, research into the effects on accessibility must first be conducted so that a balanced decision can be made.

120157.explanation: The reality is that it is difficult to entice parties to build flexible housing. Several plans are underway that, according to ONS, should be accelerated. By the way, these are not only flexible homes for young people but also for other target groups.

120159.explanation: If we want to maintain quality or improve it in places, extra money must be allocated. ONS wants to allocate a fund from the reserves aimed at giving the city’s maintenance a quality boost.

120160.explanation: ONS prefers wind turbines at sea. If we get a new obligation, we see the Haringvliet as a location to be investigated.

120100.explanation: We must strive for an equal distribution between men and women.

120101.explanation: We are strong supporters of participation and involvement. There are various means to achieve this.

120104.explanation: It is important to establish opening hours in good consultation with the hospitality industry. This is good for entrepreneurs, but also for residents and provides clarity.

120108.explanation: The legislation does not yet indicate that this is prohibited. Until then, monitoring remains necessary.

120111.explanation: It must remain possible for people with a low income and people with disabilities to have their bulky waste collected at home.

120117.explanation: It is important that children from families with low incomes can also participate. This is good for creative development.

120118.explanation: The municipality can provide temporary support, but in the long run, sports clubs must be able to stand on their own feet.

120119.explanation: Monuments contribute to the identity of our municipality and distinguish us from other places. This must be preserved.

120121.explanation: We support the current camera policy, but we believe more in prevention and physical presence.

120122.explanation: Carrying a weapon is an authority of the police.

120129.explanation: We find it important that everyone can participate. Through Voorne Putten Werkt, good steps are already being taken and many people are already contributing.

120130.explanation: We find it important that everyone can participate. People must also have a sufficient income to be able to participate.

120131.explanation: The prices of groceries are rising. More people will have problems making ends meet. If we do not raise the income threshold, these people will fall through the cracks.

120133.explanation: There must be sufficient parking spaces for electric cars. The number should be proportional to the other parking spaces. Not everyone has an electric car yet.

120134.explanation: To protect pedestrians and cyclists, we want a default maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas.

120135.explanation: We find the neighborhoods where this has now been introduced to be sufficient. There is no need to change this.

120137.explanation: We agree with the statement, but the municipality has little to no influence over care providers and their policies in this regard.

120138.explanation: When it comes to care, the quality is the foremost priority and a reasonable price must be paid for it.

120139.explanation: We especially want to make this possible for those on minimum incomes to combat period poverty.

120141.explanation: In the past period, we have abolished the dog tax for the second dog for minimum income earners. The intention is to do this for all dogs.

120143.explanation: We consider employment important. Small-scale expansions should be possible as long as they do not consume too much green space.

120145.explanation: This is a difficult choice during the housing crisis. We believe that status holders would otherwise face too much disadvantage to fully participate in society, which will later result in bigger problems. Therefore, it is wise to maintain the current policy.

120149.explanation: At the moment, there is no demand or necessity for a coffeeshop in our city.

120152.explanation: The municipality of Nissewaard has strict rules for room rentals, but it is allowed. For example, it is not permitted in corridor or apartment buildings. We support this policy.

120153.explanation: These already exist and we want to maintain and strengthen them. This is good for social cohesion.

120156.explanation: It is important to consider traffic in housing projects. Traffic plans have already been made that take housing possibilities into account. The municipality depends on other authorities such as the province and the national government for funding.

120157.explanation: Flexible housing can be part of a temporary solution to the housing shortage, but will not solve the structural problem. Current regulations also do not allow for rapid implementation. We are open to any possible solution for the housing shortage.

120159.explanation: Green spaces are important for multiple reasons. Therefore, proper maintenance of greenery is necessary. If finances permit, we can invest more in this.

120160.explanation: The current search location is west of the Hartel bridge. The PvdA wants a maximum of three wind turbines here to meet our task. The placement of wind turbines must always have support among residents and cause minimal disturbance.

120100.explanation: It is more important that the best people are in local government. This can be all men, women, or a mixture.

120101.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal is in favor of an advisory referendum. We want to know what residents think about important topics so we can take this into account in our decisions.

120104.explanation: For public order and peace in the area, it is important to have appropriate opening hours.

120108.explanation: Due to energy congestion and rising gas prices, it is important that residents can heat affordably as long as there are no affordable alternatives.

120111.explanation: We want one or two 'waste trains' per year in each neighborhood or district. This is a announced moment when Reinis, with various vehicles and teams, collects bulky waste along a route. This can optionally be combined with the successful tile removal campaign.

120117.explanation: Music lessons can be given at primary schools. Separate music lessons are then unnecessary.

120118.explanation: Sports clubs in financial difficulties should be able to turn to the municipality to seek solutions together.

120119.explanation: The local cultural history must be well supported and preserved, but Nissewaard Lokaal is critical about granting additional subsidies.

120121.explanation: Where necessary and desirable, camera surveillance may be expanded. This can be supplemented with preventive (weapon) controls. These controls help prevent violence and also make people feel safer on the streets.

120122.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal is open to a trial if desired.

120129.explanation: Money is not without conditions. In exchange for an income, a certain effort may be required if one is able to provide it.

120130.explanation: If people cannot afford it, this option must remain available. Attention must be paid to the so-called poverty trap, where people falling outside the criteria end up with less net income.

120131.explanation: Due to price increases, we see that people with higher incomes also fall into financial problems. To prevent this from worsening, we must focus on prevention before it is too late.

120133.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal wants the municipality to actively work on expanding charging points. Charging points on lampposts offer opportunities but also challenges due to grid congestion. With smart solutions, such as using solar energy, we want to enable more (fast) charging locations.

120134.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal is not in favor of a general 30 km/h speed limit for the entire municipality. With support from residents, reducing speed to 30 km/h on neighborhood access roads can be considered, provided the road design is adapted accordingly. Examples are Zuidland and Simonshaven.

120135.explanation: Parking pressure in Land and Haven is increasing. Introducing parking permits can regulate this pressure. It is important that costs for a parking permit for a first car remain limited.

120137.explanation: The municipality has no authority over this. It is the responsibility of health insurers.

120138.explanation: If support is truly needed, it must be provided carefully and based on a thorough analysis in advance. Not just looking at the child, but also the family, school, and other support systems. This prevents the accumulation of long-term care (costs).

120139.explanation: The municipality should stimulate and fund the distribution of free sanitary pads and tampons via primary and secondary schools.

120141.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal wants the dog tax to be phased out.

120143.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal wants to expand the current industrial area. It is required that new companies bring sufficient employment.

120145.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal believes local residents deserve priority; however, the reality is that we are also subject to rules from the province and national government.

120149.explanation: The establishment of a coffeeshop can cause nuisance in the area and safety issues.

120152.explanation: Room rental increases pressure on the housing market because it mainly goes to migrant workers. In addition, parking pressure increases if every room occupant owns a car.

120153.explanation: Every neighborhood and/or village should have its own community center. Where necessary, multifunctional use can be considered.

120156.explanation: New housing must go hand in hand with good accessibility within the municipality and region, such as an additional river crossing, safe roads, reliable public transport, and space for cycling.

120157.explanation: Nissewaard Lokaal wants permanent housing for starters consisting of affordable owner-occupied and social rental homes.

120159.explanation: If municipal finances allow, increasing green maintenance is important. A well-maintained outdoor space contributes to the goal of being clean, intact, and safe.

120160.explanation: This is not possible at this location due to false wind. Nissewaard Lokaal sees only the Brielse Maasdijk with three wind turbines on the west side as a serious option to contribute to the energy issue.

120100.explanation: Not gender but quality and suitability should be decisive.

120101.explanation: Residents must be able to directly participate in decision-making on important issues.

120104.explanation: Entrepreneurs should determine their own opening hours without patronizing interference.

120108.explanation: Residents should be able to decide for themselves how they heat their homes.

120111.explanation: Bulky waste collection should be free for residents.

120117.explanation: Music lessons are not the responsibility of the municipality.

120118.explanation: Support only if associations can demonstrably prove they are struggling.

120119.explanation: Cutting back on monument care is responsible, as long as monuments with clear social value are preserved. Money should not go to projects without added value, but to heritage that contributes to the community. If it turns out that too much is spent, cutting back is justified.

120122.explanation: Public order officers (boa's) must be able to defend themselves.

120129.explanation: A return service requirement with benefits is normal and ensures people continue to participate.

120130.explanation: People with low incomes should be spared.

120131.explanation: Help should be targeted, not open-ended.

120133.explanation: There are already enough charging points; additionally, charging can be done at home.

120134.explanation: No unnecessary lowering of the maximum speed limit.

120135.explanation: Parking permits make living more expensive and do not solve the parking problem.

120137.explanation: Care should be equal for everyone.

120138.explanation: Money must not be a factor in choosing the best option but should be spent wisely.

120139.explanation: There are enough possibilities available to acquire this if necessary.

120141.explanation: Is not used to address dog nuisance but disappears into the general funds. It is an unfair tax and should be abolished.

120143.explanation: Expansion only if it creates jobs for our residents.

120145.explanation: No priority for status holders in social housing.

120153.explanation: Only if residents have a need for this.

120156.explanation: Solve traffic problems first, then build.

120159.explanation: Nissewaard does a lot regarding their green spaces. We think the money available for this should simply be spent well and thoughtfully.

120160.explanation: Just no more wind turbines, and especially not in or near residential areas.

120100.explanation: It’s about the quality of the person, not the gender.

120101.explanation: Resident participation is of great importance for a reliable local democracy.

120104.explanation: Ultimately, the municipality should be decisive here but in consultation with the entrepreneurs.

120111.explanation: This prevents decline of neighborhoods and villages and will ultimately yield more money than it costs. Moreover, not everyone is able to transport bulky waste.

120117.explanation: First ensure that no children live in poverty within Nissewaard and thus ensure that basic needs are met. Music is important for children, but a good breakfast or good glasses are even more important.

120118.explanation: It is a sports club’s task to be viable by having enough paying members and sponsors. The municipality is responsible for a well-functioning facility (if it belongs to the municipality).

120119.explanation: We must be proud and careful with the limited number of monuments in Nissewaard.

120121.explanation: Current camera surveillance at known hotspots is now sufficient. The camera images must be viewed live and be of good quality.

120122.explanation: Aid workers such as public order officers (boa's) who ensure the safety of our residents must also be able to defend themselves.

120129.explanation: If (physically and mentally) permissible. Increasingly, volunteers and volunteer organizations are being called upon while there are fewer volunteers. People receiving social assistance benefits should play a meaningful role in this.

120130.explanation: If it becomes clear, after thorough assessment, that this debt forgiveness is necessary to survive, then it should be maintained. However, strong action must be taken against profiteers.

120131.explanation: The municipality of Nissewaard is already quite generous in offering assistance for poverty. This should continue, ensuring that tailored solutions are not lost sight of.

120133.explanation: This varies by location, neighborhood, and center.

120134.explanation: Not everywhere. However, in areas around schools and within the cores, yes.

120137.explanation: Healthcare is under great pressure. Everyone has the right to good care. No specific preferences can be considered here. Of course, everyone is free to choose their own healthcare provider.

120138.explanation: Quality and results always take precedence over price.

120141.explanation: The dog tax is an outdated tax that has already been abolished in most surrounding municipalities. The majority of the proceeds go towards administering this tax and do not benefit dogs and dog owners.

120143.explanation: First, ensure good accessibility to make the business climate attractive again for entrepreneurs. Additionally, this depends on the local employment generated.

120145.explanation: Status holders should not be given priority. Of course, the national government must also abolish the legal quota.

120149.explanation: A municipality with nearly 90,000 inhabitants should allow a legal coffee shop, but only in a location agreed upon by the environment, residents, and entrepreneurs. Preferably a regulated distribution point rather than more criminality in the neighborhoods.

120152.explanation: We must prevent overcrowding and oppose the arrival of even more rogue landlords.

120153.explanation: All village centers and cities within our municipality have the right to their own village center or meeting place.

120156.explanation: No new large-scale housing development plans should be developed before there is a clear and feasible plan regarding the traffic access of Nissewaard.

120157.explanation: There is huge housing shortage among the youth of Nissewaard. Flexible housing is a good solution to reduce the worst pressure. The exact number of homes needed is yet to be determined.

120159.explanation: Good green maintenance is decisive for the appearance of a municipality and prevents deterioration.

120160.explanation: We do not want wind turbines anywhere in Nissewaard.

120100.explanation: The VVD believes that the best person should be in the right position.

120101.explanation: The VVD finds it important to listen to the residents. We choose good information delivery to residents, provide participation where possible, and thereafter the municipal council (elected by the residents) decides.

120104.explanation: The VVD gives entrepreneurs the freedom to determine their own opening hours.

120108.explanation: The VVD believes residents should be able to choose how they heat their homes.

120111.explanation: The VVD wants bulky waste to be collected from home as easily as possible; however, the polluter will have to pay for this. According to the VVD, nothing is free, but we keep the costs as low as possible.

120117.explanation: The VVD believes attention to music education is a choice for schools themselves.

120118.explanation: The VVD finds sports and participation important for everyone; sports clubs are the cement of our society. We financially encourage clubs to take an active role in the municipality.

120119.explanation: The VVD believes we must keep our history and heritage alive. This includes taking good care of our monuments.

120121.explanation: The VVD wants to maintain existing camera surveillance and expand it where and when necessary.

120122.explanation: The VVD wants to equip enforcement officers with the right tools such as body cameras and pepper spray. According to the VVD, a baton is also among the options to perform their work properly and safely.

120129.explanation: The VVD believes that social assistance is not a dead end but a springboard. We help people participate again, through work or in other ways.

120130.explanation: The VVD believes municipal taxes should be paid by all residents who use municipal facilities.

120131.explanation: The VVD finds the norm of 120% of the social assistance level applied in Nissewaard too high. The income limit should actually be lowered.

120133.explanation: The VVD supports more charging points in the municipality. Double use of the parking spot is a priority to avoid increasing parking pressure in neighborhoods.

120134.explanation: The VVD believes the local traffic situation must be considered. Around schools, a maximum speed of 30 km/h is fine, but on main roads within built-up areas, 50 km/h is the norm.

120135.explanation: The VVD is cautious about expanding areas with permit parking. If there is broad support in a neighborhood, the VVD favors its introduction.

120137.explanation: The VVD believes in freedom of choice. Residents should be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits them, including preferences related to religion, culture, or beliefs.

120138.explanation: The VVD finds it important to control costs and deliver effective care. Those who really need help should have access to preventive support to prevent youth from unnecessarily entering heavy care trajectories. We select not only on price but also on quality.

120139.explanation: The VVD believes there are enough poverty regulations, making a specific measure like this unnecessary.

120141.explanation: The VVD believes the polluter must pay. However, the VVD insists that dog tax revenue is spent on dog-related matters.

120143.explanation: The VVD supports developing a new business district alongside an additional Hartel Bridge. This provides much-needed space as our region grows and employment must grow accordingly.

120145.explanation: The VVD believes status holders should not receive priority for social housing, but they believe status holders who fled their countries deserve a new home in the Netherlands. We treat them like all residents, so they do not get priority on rental housing.

120149.explanation: The VVD believes a coffee shop does not fit with our municipality. We are in favor of a safe and pleasant living environment.

120152.explanation: The VVD believes that subdividing homes leads to attracting additional labor migrants in residential neighborhoods. This currently causes nuisance in various neighborhoods. We want to counter this nuisance.

120153.explanation: The VVD is of the opinion that there are enough easily accessible meeting places near all villages. These contribute to contact between residents.

120156.explanation: The VVD wants to build 5,000 extra homes by 2035; every construction plan must include a traffic study.

120157.explanation: The VVD aims for at least 500 flexible homes in 2026. This way we can quickly offer our young people with a job a decent home.

120159.explanation: The VVD strives for an '8' grade in green maintenance. Good living and good life also mean a well-maintained outdoor space where you can enjoy recreation. With the taxes you pay to the municipality, the municipality should ensure a solid pass for the outdoor environment.

120160.explanation: The VVD wants to keep the already existing agreements. No more wind turbines will be built in Nissewaard.

120100.explanation: The CDA finds it important that men and women, people with disabilities, youth and seniors from every village and neighborhood are represented in our municipality, but we will not impose this by law.

120101.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a referendum. The municipal council is elected to make difficult trade-offs. It is important to involve citizens in other ways on important topics.

120104.explanation: For the sake of manageability of the living environment.

120108.explanation: CDA Nissewaard wants agreements about usage in densely built-up neighborhoods.

120111.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money. We pay waste disposal charges for that. The CDA wants to keep these charges as low as possible.

120117.explanation: The CDA wants sport and culture to be accessible to all children. We do this in cooperation with our associations.

120118.explanation: The CDA stands for a strong community life, but wants to focus on supporting viable associations, meaning customized solutions.

120119.explanation: It is important to preserve local heritage. That gives character and identity to our municipality.

120121.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. Extra camera surveillance can detect crime more effectively and quickly, and greatly helps the police in tackling crime.

120122.explanation: Only allow batons to be used by enforcement officers (boa's) with the specific safety task.

120129.explanation: Anyone who can should provide a social contribution in return.

120130.explanation: The municipality must show its human side: waiving municipal taxes is one of the most effective measures municipalities can take to support people with low incomes.

120131.explanation: The CDA finds it unacceptable that the municipality you live in determines to what extent you can make ends meet.

120133.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

120134.explanation: The CDA wants you to be able to drive safely at 50 km/h on the main roads within urban areas. The standard in residential streets is 30 km/h.

120137.explanation: The CDA values choice options in healthcare. Sometimes the differences in rates are so large or the quality so low that it is undesirable for every care provider to be funded by the municipality.

120138.explanation: Quality is more important than costs.

120139.explanation: But we can also leave this to social organizations; the municipality can stimulate this.

120141.explanation: However, this requires an honest discussion about compensating the municipality for this loss of income.

120143.explanation: The CDA stands for an honest and strong economy. It is important that there remains room for new business activity in our municipality.

120145.explanation: Every municipality is required to accommodate a number of status holders. Given the current housing shortage, the priority of status holders can be better balanced against other groups who also have priority for housing.

120149.explanation: Coffeeshops generally attract new criminal activity. The CDA prefers to reduce the number of coffeeshops rather than increase them.

120152.explanation: Part of society needs room rental. Think, for example, of students or young people. There must be space for this. We will combat subdivision of homes by slumlords.

120156.explanation: The current plans make a traffic plan necessary.

120157.explanation: We do see flexible homes as a good tool to stimulate the housing market.

120159.explanation: And we also want to point out initiatives by residents, for example by taking over part of the maintenance themselves.

120160.explanation: We limit the placement of new wind turbines to three wind turbines at the Brielse Maasdijk.

120100.explanation: No, members of the new college of mayor and aldermen must be chosen based on their qualities, not their gender.

120101.explanation: Yes, the residents are the voice of Nissewaard. Belang van Nissewaard thinks, listens, and acts according to the interests of the residents.

120104.explanation: Yes. Entrepreneurs already have to deal with too much bureaucracy and rules. We must give entrepreneurs more freedom, especially because Nissewaard needs to attract more visitors.

120108.explanation: Keeping your home warm is a basic necessity. The government should not decide how you keep your home warm.

120111.explanation: Nothing is free. Ultimately, the costs come directly or indirectly to the residents. Of course, Belang van Nissewaard does not want to exclude this and will look at ways to make the costs for residents transparent.

120117.explanation: There are already many free options in this area.

120118.explanation: No, because Nissewaard has enough sports clubs to facilitate sports. Moreover, the municipality already provides subsidies.

120121.explanation: We are in favor in the interest of safety.

120122.explanation: We are in favor in the interest of safety.

120129.explanation: No, this group can be easily abused. We also need to look at each individual’s circumstances, as there are various and diverse reasons why people end up on welfare.

120130.explanation: No, because we do not want to unnecessarily put people in financial trouble.

120131.explanation: First conduct research to see if it really helps and works.

120133.explanation: Agree, if there is a need for it.

120134.explanation: We are against 30 km/h zones within urban areas because it slows traffic flow without significantly improving safety on all roads.

120135.explanation: The costs of car ownership and use are already high, and this also impacts social cohesion.

120137.explanation: Due to shortages, we do not agree with this.

120138.explanation: Always watch the price. Efficiency is top priority in everything.

120141.explanation: It is a disproportionate tax. You also do not pay taxes for cats, geese, or other animals.

120143.explanation: Yes, provided that this does not result in extra traffic pressure.

120145.explanation: No, priority for our own residents.

120149.explanation: Yes, for safety and to prevent illegal sales on the street.

120152.explanation: This is an approach that can help solve the housing shortage. Landlords should also be able to make their own choice in this.

120153.explanation: Yes, the village center is an important part of the residents' quality of life.

120156.explanation: Yes, we consider this a logical regulation and a logical step in business matters.

120157.explanation: Agreed. Belang van Nissewaard wants more attention for building homes for young people, especially for local youth.

120159.explanation: No, because this is already well maintained.

120160.explanation: No, Belang van Nissewaard is against wind turbines and is open to their removal where possible.

120100.explanation: For Jong Nissewaard, the gender of an alderman is unimportant. We find it essential that an alderman is chosen based on qualities relevant to the position rather than irrelevant (personal) characteristics.

120101.explanation: Referendums strengthen local democracy because residents can directly influence council decisions collectively. Additionally, referendums promote citizen participation, government transparency, and the legitimacy of council decisions.

120104.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to run their businesses. It is not the government's task to intervene in the opening hours of cafes and restaurants. These hours affect turnover, and the municipality should stimulate economic growth, especially of local entrepreneurs.

120108.explanation: Jong Nissewaard believes the municipality should not interfere behind people's front doors. Therefore, we will not actively oppose the use of fireplaces and wood stoves, as for some people this is their way to stay warm in winter and create a cozy atmosphere.

120111.explanation: Free bulk waste collection at home means costs will rise elsewhere. This will burden residents who produce less waste. We find this an unfair distribution. Jong Nissewaard does want to remove the maximum number of times residents can bring waste to the depot.

120117.explanation: If every primary school child receives a municipal subsidy, this means even the strongest shoulders are supported. We find this unnecessary. Moreover, it costs the municipality a lot of money and this financial space is not available.

120118.explanation: Sports clubs should be able to stand on their own feet as much as possible. When clubs are at risk of failing, the government can provide small support. We prefer that money is spent on improving and constructing outdoor sports facilities.

120119.explanation: Currently, not much money goes to monument care. Cutting budgets would mean monuments risk decay. This would not be a good development because monuments are places for remembrance and relaxation.

120121.explanation: More camera surveillance at hotspots reduces the chance of nuisance. At the same time, we should not be blind to displacement effects. Camera surveillance is therefore one part of a broad approach, which also includes preventive searches, deploying more (youth) enforcement officers, and prevention.

120122.explanation: Jong Nissewaard believes that whether or not to equip enforcement officers with batons is a matter for national politics. At the municipal level, it is mainly important to expand enforcement capacity.

120129.explanation: People receiving social assistance already have a work obligation. They are therefore already encouraged to work. An additional obligation is unnecessary and distracts them from their development. People on social assistance often want to get out of it themselves.

120130.explanation: Residents with low incomes should be encouraged to develop themselves. By forgiving taxes for this specific group, you reward people for having a lower income. Jong Nissewaard believes that people should be rewarded for working harder instead.

120131.explanation: Many residents in Nissewaard struggle to make ends meet. We want local facilities for people living in poverty to be strengthened. For this, we can raise the income threshold so more people qualify for these facilities and we improve basic security.

120133.explanation: The number of plug-in cars is rapidly increasing. There must therefore be sufficient (semi-)public charging points. If this is not the case, it can be a barrier to choosing an electric car, while this is an important sustainable step in the right direction.

120134.explanation: Limiting the maximum speed is a matter of customization, especially because it is not always clear or correct what exactly counts as within built-up areas. If lowering the maximum speed is necessary to improve traffic safety, Jong Nissewaard is in favor.

120135.explanation: Jong Nissewaard is generally against increasing costs for residents. A parking permit will also result in more expenses for residents, and we do not consider that a good move. We also do not want to punish car ownership.

120137.explanation: Too many people want care that fits their orientation, while their care needs can often be solved more generally. We want to move towards care facilities that are qualitatively appropriate rather than having a separate care facility for each individual's orientation or personal characteristics.

120138.explanation: In youth care, it's too often about costs. We believe it should be about the quality of care. Quality also includes the reliability of a youth care partner. Supervision and enforcement of fraudulent care providers is just as important as selection beforehand.

120139.explanation: This is not a core task of government. Nonetheless, we recognize how expensive tampons and sanitary pads can be, especially for residents with a lower socioeconomic status. For that reason, we support initiatives from organizations, such as schools and associations, to provide these products.

120141.explanation: Jong Nissewaard believes the abolition of the dog tax is a matter for national politics. As long as the dog tax remains a purpose tax and the money collected is actually used for dog owners, we oppose its abolition.

120143.explanation: The workforce in Nissewaard is expected to decline. Also, the competitive position compared to the Rotterdam-Rijnmond region could improve. Expanding business parks ensures that Nissewaard can grow economically and that entrepreneurs have space to earn more money.

120145.explanation: Jong Nissewaard is generally against priority allocation for social rental housing. Everyone should have equal chances to obtain a home regardless of background. The priority scheme for status holders must therefore also be abolished.

120149.explanation: A coffeeshop addresses part of the illegal market. In a coffeeshop, better quality soft drugs are also sold than on the street. We do want maximum opening hours, enforcement of nuisance, and controls.

120152.explanation: This encourages landlords who exploit tenants. Moreover, it gives a free pass to house large groups of labor migrants in one location.

120153.explanation: The cores/villages in our municipality have a special character. They form the counterpart to the urban Spijkenisse. Jong Nissewaard therefore supports initiatives from residents to have their own village center.

120156.explanation: Nissewaard has a major accessibility problem. We want a Second Hartel Bridge to address this. At the same time, we cannot wait to build new homes. It is an 'and-and' story. The challenges of housing and accessibility must be addressed simultaneously.

120157.explanation: Jong Nissewaard wants to diversify the housing stock as much as possible with different forms of housing to promote flow. More flexible housing for young people contributes to this.

120159.explanation: The street scene is significantly damaged. This reduces the (subjective) safety of the living environment. We will reverse austerity measures on maintenance so that streets are clean, intact, and safe again. This also allows more attention to combating litter nuisance.

120160.explanation: As far as Jong Nissewaard is concerned, wind energy is an outdated method for generating sustainable energy. It is expensive and generally dangerous and unreliable. This does not fit with our climate-realistic attitude.

120100.explanation: The composition of the college should not depend on gender but on quality.

120101.explanation: The municipal council is the political body representing the people, setting the framework, and exercising oversight in the municipality. This makes a referendum unnecessary.

120104.explanation: The municipality must take the lead in this.

120108.explanation: Provided this does not cause unnecessary pollution and nuisance.

120111.explanation: Nothing is free, so a low waste disposal tax is desirable.

120117.explanation: That would be nice, but it will probably be very costly.

120118.explanation: The municipality can certainly look at a struggling club with empathy, but it is also important that an association or club can stand on its own two feet.

120121.explanation: Enforcement of public order & safety is a top priority for the ChristenUnie-SGP.

120129.explanation: If they are physically able, this could be possible and desirable for both parties.

120130.explanation: Some of our residents really need this waiver.

120131.explanation: The current income limit is sufficient at this moment but must be continuously monitored.

120133.explanation: Fortunately, more and more charging stations are being added in the municipality.

120134.explanation: In areas where it is desirable and necessary, yes, but it may not be possible everywhere.

120135.explanation: The layout of the town center core and surrounding ring, and the policy regarding parking permits related to this, are currently in order.

120138.explanation: Careful consideration must be given to selecting based on a good price/quality ratio.

120141.explanation: ChristenUnie-SGP supports abolishing the dog tax in the coming council period.

120143.explanation: If possible, but not at the expense of green areas and agricultural land.

120149.explanation: Coffeeshops generally cause a lot of nuisance in the municipality and are therefore undesirable!

120152.explanation: This is also a possibility to reduce the housing shortage.

120156.explanation: Building new homes must go hand in hand with good accessibility to Nissewaard municipality. So do both!

120159.explanation: The green maintenance in the municipality is not in order everywhere, so in some places more maintenance is needed.

120160.explanation: For ChristenUnie-SGP, only three wind turbines west of the Hartelbrug are negotiable.

120100.explanation: GroenLinks believes that the government should be a good reflection of society. This is not only about men and women but also about different backgrounds, ages, and experiences. This makes decisions fairer and better for all residents of Nissewaard.

120101.explanation: Residents must be able to participate in important decisions. The municipality is there for the residents and must listen to them. A referendum can help with this, provided it is organized properly and fairly and the costs are not too high.

120104.explanation: Lively neighborhoods are important, with room for hospitality businesses. But opening hours must fit the neighborhood: residents should also be able to live peacefully. Therefore: tailor-made per location, together with residents and entrepreneurs.

120108.explanation: GroenLinks wants clean air and healthy neighborhoods. Smoke from wood stoves causes fine particles and nuisance, especially for children and people with lung problems. Therefore, we want to limit their use and focus on cleaner, sustainable heating methods.

120111.explanation: GroenLinks wants to reduce waste and promote reuse. Free collection can help prevent litter but must not lead to more waste. We choose a fair system that encourages reuse and waste separation.

120117.explanation: Every child must be able to participate, even if their parents have little money. Music lessons help development and self-confidence, so subsidies are a good investment.

120118.explanation: Associations must be able to continue existing because they are important for social contact and health. But direct financial support is not always the best solution. The municipality prefers to help with customized support.

120119.explanation: Investing in monuments and special buildings. Renovating is better and more sustainable than demolishing. Monuments are part of Nissewaard’s identity and provide culture, pride, and a beautiful living environment.

120121.explanation: We actually want fewer cameras because they often provide false security. If cameras are needed, they must be temporary, well explained, and clearly signposted. Safety is mainly achieved with visible community police officers and good neighborhood watch.

120122.explanation: Safety is important, but without unnecessary hardening. We choose recognizable community police officers, youth workers, and enforcement officers in the neighborhood, not more weapons. Good training, cooperation, and de-escalation work better than a baton.

120129.explanation: People on social assistance must be treated respectfully. The rules should be applied generously. Participation is important but should not be a punishment. Volunteer work can be done as long as it fits a person’s situation and increases opportunities. But it must remain voluntary.

120130.explanation: People with low incomes must receive support. The municipality must be generous with minimum incomes and actively combat poverty. Stopping waivers increases poverty. Therefore, we continue to support this scheme.

120131.explanation: No one must remain in poverty. The municipality must be generous with social assistance and actively support minimum income groups. If the income limit is too low, people fall through the cracks. That limit should therefore be raised.

120133.explanation: We want to accelerate the transition to sustainable transport. Therefore, there must be enough charging points so that electric driving is possible for everyone. At the same time, we want to preserve green spaces and create more room for bicycles and public transport.

120134.explanation: We want more traffic safety and cleaner air. A 30 km/h speed limit in built-up areas leads to fewer accidents, less noise, and makes cycling and walking safer. This fits our plan for livable neighborhoods with more space for green.

120135.explanation: Reduce the number of cars in the neighborhood and provide more space for greenery and playgrounds. A parking permit can help reduce parking pressure but is not an end in itself. We choose a solution per neighborhood that fits the residents and livability.

120137.explanation: Everyone is welcome and the municipality must proactively ensure equal treatment. Care must be tailored to people. If faith or culture is important, there should be space for it, as long as the quality is good, it is feasible, and no one is excluded.

120138.explanation: Good youth care must be the priority. The municipality should focus on quality, prevention, and support for families. Only looking at the lowest price is wrong: it’s about good care that truly helps children.

120139.explanation: No one should be excluded because of poverty. The municipality must actively support low-income households and combat poverty. Free menstrual products help against 'menstrual poverty' and ensure everyone can participate. We also consider sustainable alternatives.

120141.explanation: The dog tax is a designated tax. The proceeds are used for dog facilities. If the tax is abolished, the money must come from somewhere else.

120143.explanation: We want to preserve space for nature and green areas, such as maintaining the Wolvenpolder as a nature reserve. Growth must not come at the expense of livability, nature, and clean air. The current industrial sites are sufficient.

120145.explanation: People without housing, such as status holders, must be assisted fairly. Priority is sometimes necessary, just like for people after a divorce or homelessness. At the same time, enough affordable homes for everyone must be added.

120149.explanation: We are open to the establishment of new coffeeshops. Good regulation reduces street dealing and increases safety. If it is carefully managed and fits within the neighborhood, this can contribute to an open and well-controlled policy. However, supply must be legal.

120152.explanation: We want affordable housing, especially for young people and starters. Renting rooms can help alleviate housing shortage. However, clear rules must be in place to prevent nuisance and exploitative landlords and protect neighborhood livability.

120153.explanation: We want livable neighborhoods with meeting places such as community centers or libraries. Not every village needs the same facilities, but every area should have a place where people can meet. Tailored solutions per village, town, or neighborhood come first.

120156.explanation: Housing construction and accessibility must go hand in hand. We urgently need affordable homes, especially for youth, starters, and seniors. Therefore: build simultaneously and create a good traffic plan, so neighborhoods remain livable and safe. We focus mainly on sustainable transport such as public transport, cycling, and walking.

120157.explanation: It is time for many more affordable homes for young people and starters. Flexible housing can help with the housing shortage. However, they must be of good quality and not replace permanent affordable housing construction.

120159.explanation: More greenery in every neighborhood: trees, green playgrounds, and fewer tiles. Proper maintenance must also be arranged. Greenery provides shade, cooling, better water drainage, and a more pleasant living environment. But green spaces must also be allowed to grow and not be mowed too often. Let the dandelion stand.

120160.explanation: We have agreements about wind energy and must honor them.

120100.explanation: Equal representation contributes to balanced decision-making, provided quality always comes first.

120101.explanation: Referenda increase citizen involvement, provided they are used carefully and not too often.

120104.explanation: The municipality must set clear frameworks to prevent nuisance and insecurity, with room for customized solutions via permits.

120108.explanation: It is possible as long as no structural nuisance or health problems arise.

120111.explanation: A periodic free collection day is desirable, with tailored solutions for people without transport.

120118.explanation: Sports clubs play an important social role.

120119.explanation: Monuments are cultural heritage and deserve protection.

120121.explanation: Camera surveillance can be useful, but only if it is used effectively.

120122.explanation: Armament increases tensions and is not inherently necessary.

120129.explanation: Only stimulate, do not impose forcefully.

120130.explanation: Only income-dependent. People below the poverty line should be exempted.

120131.explanation: That only leads to more poverty.

120133.explanation: Simply more charging stations with double connections need to be installed where charging is also possible during nighttime hours. The municipality can allocate more subsidies to reduce congestion.

120134.explanation: This increases traffic safety and livability.

120137.explanation: Good care must be accessible to everyone based on quality.

120138.explanation: Quality and continuity are more important.

120141.explanation: Abolishing it costs the municipality money while dogs cause additional pollution.

120143.explanation: First better utilization of existing sites.

120145.explanation: The municipality must take its legal responsibility.

120152.explanation: Provided that it does not mean that ALL rooms in such a house are rented out separately. To avoid giving housing speculators such an idea.

120153.explanation: Only if there is demonstrable need.

120157.explanation: This offers young people a quick perspective.

120159.explanation: In any case, do not cut back further on green maintenance.

120160.explanation: In the coming years, we will need increasingly more electric energy. If we don't do it, the energy bill will rise even faster, because the generated power that must be transported from the North Sea to the municipality of Nissewaard also has to be paid for through network company tariffs.

120100.explanation: It concerns the capacity of a person.

120101.explanation: Important decisions must have broad support among the residents.

120104.explanation: Cafe and restaurant owners are entrepreneurs and should be able to decide for themselves when they are open. That is part of free enterprise. What works in a city center does not necessarily work in a residential area. Local entrepreneurs can adjust their hours to their environment.

120108.explanation: The use of fireplaces and wood stoves has both advantages and disadvantages. They provide comfort and atmosphere but can also cause nuisance and health risks. The municipality must also adhere to the burning alert from the RIVM.

120111.explanation: For example, once a month. Preferably at the underground and container collection point.

120117.explanation: Municipal money can only be spent once. Other matters such as healthcare, poverty reduction, and basic education have priority. This should also fall under education!

120118.explanation: No guarantee of a structural solution. Financial support often only solves temporary problems and does not prevent the association from encountering difficulties again later.

120119.explanation: Shared responsibility. Owners, funds, and the municipality can together ensure preservation, instead of the municipality paying for everything.

120121.explanation: Faster identification of offenders. Camera footage helps police and enforcers to identify and track suspects. Greater sense of security. Residents often feel safer in places with camera surveillance, such as shopping centers and entertainment areas, and it also has a preventive function.

120122.explanation: Boys (special enforcement officers) are not police. Their task is mainly supervision and enforcement, not the use of force. A baton does not always fit that role. Training in conflict management, communication, and cooperation with the police can be more effective than arming them.

120129.explanation: Unless it is safe for both the aid worker and recipient. It also has to suit the person. So, customization and supervision are appropriate here.

120130.explanation: Residents with a low income often struggle to make ends meet. Forgiveness prevents them from falling further into debt. Municipalities have the task of combating poverty and supporting vulnerable residents.

120131.explanation: Nowadays, even dual earners struggle to make ends meet due to high housing costs, groceries, and childcare. Energy poverty affects middle incomes. Rising energy prices cause households with a 'just too high' income to still face payment problems.

120133.explanation: Designating more parking spaces as charging spots can reduce available parking for other residents. As long as demand is limited and alternatives exist, expansion is unnecessary and can actually increase parking pressure. Large charging stations outside the municipality.

120134.explanation: Lowering the maximum speed to 30 km/h within built-up areas increases traffic safety, reduces noise nuisance, and makes streets more livable for residents, cyclists, pedestrians, and animals.

120135.explanation: Moves the problem. Parking problems do not disappear but shift to surrounding neighborhoods without permits. If it concerns a disabled parking permit, this must be treated with high priority.

120137.explanation: Choosing a healthcare provider is important but must be balanced with availability, quality, and affordability of care, so that the healthcare system remains accessible and feasible for everyone.

120138.explanation: When selecting youth care, not only price matters but also quality, continuity, and suitable support. A careful consideration of multiple factors is necessary to ensure good assistance for young people.

120139.explanation: Several foundations already provide care here, such as the poverty fund. The municipality can better deploy the money. It is also a basic need, so the municipality can actively work on cooperation with foundations. Perhaps there is also a role here for health insurers.

120141.explanation: It is a medieval tax, still collected by only one-third of all Dutch municipalities. Abolishing the dog tax leads to fairer burdens for dog owners and reduces administrative costs, while owning a dog is not additionally financially penalized.

120143.explanation: Expanding business parks offers economic opportunities as they are the backbone of our society. The limited space should not be made available to large distribution centers.

120145.explanation: Priority for refugees with a residence permit may be perceived as unfair by other housing seekers who have been waiting a long time. Equal treatment and a fair distribution of scarce social rental housing are important.

120149.explanation: The establishment of new coffeeshops can have advantages, such as better distribution and regulation, but also disadvantages like possible nuisance. A careful consideration of local circumstances and enforceability is therefore necessary. Medical marijuana can already be obtained safely.

120152.explanation: Allowing room-by-room rental can provide additional housing but also poses risks to livability and pressure on neighborhoods. Therefore, this is only responsible with strict rules and clear restrictions, for example limited to specific situations such as informal care.

120153.explanation: Having a village center strengthens social cohesion, provides space for meetings and activities, and increases livability and resident involvement in each village.

120156.explanation: Drawing up a traffic plan first is necessary because the current access is already under pressure. This way, housing construction can be better aligned with accessibility, safety, and livability.

120157.explanation: But not exclusively for young people; there are also other groups urgently looking for housing.

120159.explanation: More investment in green maintenance improves livability, biodiversity, and residents' health and ensures an attractive and well-maintained public space.

120160.explanation: New wind turbines, including along the Spui, have a large impact on the landscape, living environment, health, and support. Given the nuisance and limited space, further placement of wind turbines in Nissewaard is undesirable.

statements.title: Nissewaard must first draw up a traffic plan and only then build new homes in Spijkenisse.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents of Nissewaard must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Nissewaard moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Hundreds of temporary flex homes for young people must urgently be provided in Nissewaard.

titleParts.glossary: Flexible, small homes for people who need housing quickly.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Nissewaard.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Nissewaard should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Nissewaard controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
Nissewaard should introduce parking permits in more neighbourhoods.

titleParts.text: Nissewaard moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Nissewaard should invest more money in green-space maintenance.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
In Nissewaard the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Nissewaard moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
Industrial/business parks in Nissewaard should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: New wind turbines may only be placed along the Spui, opposite Nieuw-Beijerland and Piershil.

titleParts.text: Alleen langs het Spui, tegenover Nieuw-Beijerland en Piershil, mogen nog nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
